mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-decline months.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger negative risk sentiment and downward S&P 500 price adjustments within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE changes are below the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month expansions in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand strength, which drives corporate earnings revisions and sustains positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or walk-forward Sharpe ratio drops below 0.5 over 3-year out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices during post-shock recovery phases indicate capacity utilization improvements and supply-side stabilization, which reliably precede positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism reverses direction during inflationary supply constraints, or forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10) in recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth above the long-term trend directly reflects macroeconomic expansion that supports corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days following data releases.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >50% of above-trend GDP quarters, or the signal exhibits zero predictive IC after controlling for market momentum.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price inflation above 3.5% annually forces markets to price in aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in >65% of high-inflation months, or the signal fails to predict returns when inflation expectations remain anchored.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and declines in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening that boosts consumer spending expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal loses predictive power when wage growth decouples from employment, or forward returns are negative in >55% of co-movement instances.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate increases during late-cycle tightening phases trigger liquidity withdrawal and higher discount rates, which immediately suppress equity valuations and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns when rate hikes are fully priced in, or forward returns are positive in >60% of tightening months.
